business.headline: Centre eyes LIC, PSU stake sales to cushion strain from oil crisis: ReportThe Centre is speeding up stake sales in LIC, Hindustan Zinc and other state-run firms. The move is aimed at creating fiscal room as oil price volatility pressures government finances.

Sensex jumps 579 points as IT stocks shine; Nifty closes above 24,175The BSE Sensex climbed 579.48 points, or 0.75%, to close at 77,502.12, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 169.85 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 24,175.70.

AI rally cracks: Why Asian markets are crashing while Indian IT stocks rallyAsian chip stocks slumped after reports stoked fears of excess AI computing capacity. Indian IT shares, more tied to software spending, rose as investors weighed a different AI demand story.

Want to save more tax under new regime? Ask your employer about this NPS benefitWith ITR filing in full swing, our 'KNOW YOUR TAX' series helps you understand the tax benefits that still matter. This edition explains how an employer's NPS contribution can help salaried employees save tax while building a retirement corpus.
Mehli Mistry steps down from Ratan Tata's family officeMistry's resignation from RNT Associates comes months after he was removed from Tata Trusts in November 2025.

EPFO new rule: PF contribution above Rs 1,800 now voluntary; what it means for youThe updated rules are expected to make the EPF system simpler, more digital and easier to use for nearly 8 crore active EPFO subscribers. The new scheme came into effect on June 29 after it was published in the Gazette, replacing the long-standing EPF Scheme, 1952.

Why are IT stocks rising today? Infosys, HCLTech, TCS jump up to 5%Infosys, HCLTech, TCS rise: IT stocks rebounded sharply on Thursday, led by Infosys and other large-cap names. The recovery reflected a shift from overheated AI chip plays to software firms seen as steadier AI beneficiaries.

Rs 35,000-crore investment push linked to Maruti plant during Japan PM's visitThe Kharkhoda plant is Maruti Suzuki's fourth manufacturing facility in India and is expected to significantly boost the country's passenger vehicle production capacity.

Sensex jumps nearly 400 points, Nifty tops 24,100 as IT stocks reboundIT stocks led the rally, while falling crude oil prices and easing geopolitical concerns boosted investor confidence. However, a weak monsoon remains a key concern.

OTT subscriptions becoming a trap? 8 in 10 users say they're being manipulatedA nationwide survey has found that eight in ten OTT users in India say they have encountered 'dark patterns', deceptive design practices that nudge users into spending more, continuing subscriptions or making choices they may not have intended.

Energy crisis: Is the worst now over? Sajjid Chinoy respondsPrices of commercial LPG were cut by Rs 183.50 per 19-kg cylinder on Wednesday – the first reduction this year following a series of hikes in the wake of the West Asia crisis that caused energy supply disruptions across the globe.

Red Bull, PepsiCo, Sting among 6 energy drink brands get FSSAI noticesThe FSSAI issued notices to six energy drink brands -- Red Bull Energy Drink, PepsiCo's Adrenaline Rush Energy Drink, Reliance Consumer Products' Campa Energy Drink Gold Boost, Sting Energy Drink, Hell Energy and Coca-Cola-backed Monster Energy -- over misbranding and misleading claims.

PPF, NSC, SCSS interest rates for July-September 2026 declared: Has anything changed?The Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) and Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) will continue to offer 8.2%, the highest among the major small savings schemes.

Indian women are taking charge of their money. Here's how they are investingThe image of women as only careful savers is fast changing. Today, more Indian women are investing, exploring digital platforms and taking control of their financial future.

EPFO extends online service outage till July 2: What does it mean for members?EPAs part of the upgrade, members will not be able to submit fresh claims through the EPFO portal during the maintenance period. The organisation has also temporarily stopped the processing of claims while the migration work is underway.

Sensex rises over 440 points, Nifty closes above 24,000; IT stocks cap gainsFinancial, auto and FMCG stocks powered the rally, while weakness in IT shares and concerns over a weak monsoon kept investors cautious.

RBI's latest worry isn't inflation. It's artificial intelligenceArtificial intelligence is no longer just transforming banking—it is emerging as one of the biggest threats to the financial system. In its latest Financial Stability Report, the RBI has warned that AI-powered cyberattacks, dependence on common AI platforms and growing technology risks could pose fresh challenges for banks and financial institutions.

IndiGo's new budget-friendly 'Lite' fare for cabin baggage flyers takes off July 15According to the airline, the fare is designed for passengers travelling with only cabin baggage. By paying only for the services they use, customers can have greater control over their travel costs.

A rare NRI investment opportunity, lost in paperwork? Nithin Kamath thinks soThe Zerodha co-founder says lengthy onboarding processes may prevent NRIs from taking advantage of attractive FCNR deposit rates enabled by a recent RBI move.

8th Pay Commission: From Rs 18,000 to Rs 69,000? Minimum pay debate explainedEmployee organisations believe the current minimum basic pay of Rs 18,000, introduced nearly a decade ago under the 7th Pay Commission, no longer reflects the cost of living. Depending on the method used, unions have proposed increasing it to anywhere between Rs 55,000 and nearly Rs 69,000.

entertainment.headline: Alpha box office Day 1 prediction: Alia Bhatt's film may open below Raazi, Jigra Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha is heading to theatres with muted advance booking numbers. The early trend points to a modest opening for the YRF Spy Universe's first female-led thriller.

Isakapatnam review: Aishwarya Rajesh's crime drama is familiar, flawed but engagingRelease Date:Thu Jul 02, 2026 Isakapatnam review: Set against the backdrop of a coastal town, Isakapatnam is a grounded crime drama that thrives on power struggles, shifting loyalties and strong performances. It may not reinvent the genre, but it tells a familiar story with enough conviction to keep you hooked till the end.

Richa Chadha tears into Dia Mirza trolls, says critics can 'bully a 5-year-old' Richa Chadha defended Dia Mirza after a podcast clip about her son Avyaan triggered online trolling. Her response shifted attention to the backlash, the criticism of a five-year-old, and Dia's environmental advocacy.

Why Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce's New York wedding is America's royal moment Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's reported New York wedding is shaping up to be America's biggest celebrity event. From the rumoured Madison Square Garden venue to the star-studded guest list, here's everything we know.

Stebin Ben weighs in on Kriti Sanon's wedding speculation and Kabir Bahia rumours Stebin Ben has addressed the ongoing speculation around Kriti Sanon's marriage amid dating rumours with Kabir Bahia. His comments offer a fresh perspective on the actor's personal life and why there should be no rush.

Project Hail Mary OTT release: When and where to watch Ryan Gosling's sci-fi film Amazon MGM Studios has announced that Project Hail Mary will premiere on Prime Video on 3 July and released a new clip. The Ryan Gosling-led adaptation follows Ryland Grace as he tries to solve the mystery behind the dying sun, with an unexpected friendship reshaping the mission.

Jana Nayagan leak case: 1.2 crore watched Vijay film illegally, police tells court Chennai police told the Madras High Court that a leaked copy of Vijay's Jana Nayagan was watched by 1.2 crore people before certification and release. The submission came as the court rejected bail for two accused in the Jana Nayagan leak case.

Director Abhinay Deo on his films called ahead of time: Never understood the label Director Abhinay Deo said he never understood why some of his films are labelled "ahead of their time." In an exclusive chat with India Today, he also recalled convincing Karisma Kapoor to star in Brown and shared an update on a possible 24 revival.

Actor Jean Paul Lal on Balan success: It's fantastic, especially as a nepo kid Actor Jean Paul Lal has spoken about the success of Balan The Boy and his father Lal's emotional response to the film. He said the appreciation feels amazing, especially as a nepo kid.

Enola Holmes 3 review: A sunlit mystery that forgets its own sparkRelease Date:Wed Jul 01, 2026 Enola Holmes 3 review: Millie Bobby Brown returns as the young detective in Netflix's latest mystery adventure alongside Henry Cavill and Louis Partridge. Does the third film live up to the franchise's charm? Read our spoiler-free review to know more.

Heart Beat season 3 release date out: All we know about the show JioHotstar has announced that Heart Beat season 3 will premiere later this month. The update also clears speculation over the show's lead character Reena's return.

What to watch on OTT this week? Obsession, Isakapatnam and more across platforms Streaming platforms are rolling out a varied slate of films and series this week, led by Rajkumar Hirani's OTT debut and Malayalam cinema's first musical horror comedy. The line-up spans supernatural, crime, comedy and folk horror, giving viewers a broad mix of new choices.

Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce: Here's how much the New York wedding may cost Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's reported Madison Square Garden wedding is making headlines for its staggering estimated cost. Here's what experts say about the multi-million-dollar celebration.

Pregnant Samantha shot Maa Inti Bangaaram car chase, director calls her superstar Director Nandini Reddy said Samantha Ruth Prabhu filmed the Thassadiya song and a car chase sequence for Maa Inti Bangaaram during her pregnancy. She called her a superstar as the film is heading towards Rs 100-crore mark globally.

I'm an artiste, not politician: Diljit Dosanjh reacts to fan query on CJP protest During a recent Instagram Live session, singer-actor Diljit Dosanjh was asked about the ongoing Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar.

Uorfi Javed visits Kamakhya temple days after saying she's atheist Uorfi Javed's latest visit to the Kamakhya Devi temple has reignited online conversations after she recently addressed viral conversion rumours. Here's what happened.

What's new on Netflix this week? Top films and shows to watch Netflix is adding a mix of comedy, thriller, anime and family drama to its July lineup. Check out the biggest movies and series premiering over the next few days.

Singer Rabbi Shergill defends Rahman's communal bias remark: How can anyone deny it? Rabbi Shergill said AR Rahman's suggestion of a communal angle in entertainment cannot be dismissed. He linked the issue to propaganda cinema, film financing and a wider ideological shift.

general news.headline: Delhi has 9,000 charging points, targets 32,000: Rekha Gupta on EV policy rolloutDelhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta said Delhi's EV charging network will expand from 9,000 to 32,000 points, assuring residents of hassle-free charging under the new EV Policy.

Gujarat couple, daughter killed in fire at Ohio motel where they livedThe family locked themselves inside a bathroom at the motel in an attempt to escape the fire, but all there died of suffocation before they could be rescued.

AI, defence, energy: PM Modi, Takaichi widen India-Japan partnershipAI, semiconductors, defence and energy dominated the agenda, signalling that India and Japan are looking well beyond traditional cooperation and preparing for a future shaped by technology and economic security.

In China, rain falls from buildings to defeat rising heat. How does it work?China's rooftop misting system is going viral for cooling buildings during heatwaves. Here's how evaporative cooling helps lower temperatures by up to 8°C.

How Vijay govt is saving big for Tamil Nadu, and contractors are thanking himTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay-led government's decision to open even the smallest public works projects for civic tender has resulted in contractors quoting sharply lower rates, resulting in big gains for the state's exchequer.

The new scheme came into effect on June 29 after it was published in the Gazette, replacing the long-standing EPF Scheme, 1952.

Chetan Bhagat's viral 'regressive baniya family' theory behind Siya-Ketan caseIn an interview with India Today, Chetan Bhagat said family shame lay at the centre of the Pune murder case. The case, which involved the gruesome murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal by his fiancee Siya and her boyfriend, has whipped up a massive media frenzy.

BJP grapples with wave of factionalism even as Karnataka polls draw nearKarnataka's politics has always been dominated by factionalism and internal party rivalries. This virus afflicted the Congress for nearly three years as DK Shivakumar and Siddaramaiah tussled for the CM's chair. Now, the virus of internal turmoil appears to have spread to the opposition BJP.

IITian chooses peace over pay, rejects Rs 32 LPA Gurgaon job to stay in BengaluruAn IIT Roorkee graduate and data scientist has gone viral after rejecting a Rs 32 LPA job offer in Gurgaon to stay in Bengaluru. Citing safety, peace of mind and quality of life, her decision has sparked a wider debate on what truly defines a successful career.

Huge potholes on Delhi-Dehradun highway spark outrage, NHAI shifts blameA video has gone viral showing two large potholes on the Delhi-Dehradun Expressway, which was inaugurated around three months ago by PM Modi. The viral video has put the Rs 1,200 crore highway under scrutiny.

First he cheats, then calls: Old video shows Siya abusing on phone while partyingAn old video has surfaced showing Siya Goyal drinking at a pub and using abusive language during a heated phone call, in which she is heard saying, "First he cheats on me, then he calls me."

Why can't India build a decent visa website, asks The Economist. Indians respondThe Economist's criticism of India's visa and other government websites has sparked strong reactions on social media. While some slammed the London-based magazine for its alleged biased views, others agreed with its opinion, and said the user experience needed to be improved.

15-year-old made to wait: What exactly is India's plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi?India tour of England: Fast-tracked into the senior squad following a historic IPL campaign, the decision to leave 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench invites a compelling question over India's delicate balance between hierarchy and current merit.

Live: PM Takaichi bats for stronger India-Japan partnership amid global uncertaintyJapan PM India Visit Live Updates: In her first ever trip to India, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi arrived in New Delhi for a three-day official visit. Accompanied by PM Narendra Modi, Taikaichi received a ceremonial welcome at the Rashtrapati Bhavan with a Guard of Honour. She then proceeded to the Hyderabad House, where she participated in a bilateral meeting with PM Modi. The bilateral talks were followed by the exchange of MoUs and press statements. Takaichi will the address the 13th annual India-Japan Business Forum at Hotel Taj Palace at 3 pm. The Japanese leader is scheduled to depart for Tokyo on Friday morning. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Kerala man, UP woman found dead in rented room under suspicious circumstancesA Kerala hospital employee and a local woman were found dead under suspicious circumstances in a locked rented room in Uttar Pradesh's Saharanpur, with police suspecting the deaths may be linked to a relationship.

This mini electric SUV is set to hit Indian roads this yearThe VinFast VF 3, set to arrive in India later this year, aims to bring affordable urban electric mobility with compact dimensions, SUV-inspired styling and a claimed driving range of up to 210 km.

The new face of female action in South cinema: Samantha, Priety and AbhiramiWhat makes Maa Inti Bangaaram and Blast stand out isn't that women are performing the action, but that they do so without sacrificing character, emotion or believability.

Watch: Bihar board exam papers found floating in rainwater, viral video sparks rowBundles of Bihar Board question papers and answer sheets were seen floating in rainwater at a Buxar education centre before quarterly exams for Classes 9, 10 and 12. The viral visuals have triggered criticism, though officials insist the examination material was not damaged and exams will continue as scheduled.

Supreme Court junks tribunal order for using citations hallucinated by AIThe Supreme Court has directed the Bar Council of India to form an expert committee to examine artificial intelligence in adjudication after finding tribunals had relied on fabricated AI-generated judicial precedents.

Elderly man drowns after falling into manhole amid rain; 4 officials suspendedA 60-year-old pedestrian fell into an open manhole on Khairani Road during drainage repairs and died. The BMC has announced action against the contractor as police examine safety lapses at the site.

Beyond mileage, E20 is accelerating wear and tear in older petrol carsMileage was the first warning sign. Now, a survey suggests prolonged use of E20 petrol could also be accelerating wear and tear in older, non-E20-compatible petrol vehicles.

Live-in, woman who turned to be a man, stalking: Story of a Rajkot murderA 20-year-old man has been arrested for allegedly murdering his live-in partner in Gujarat's Rajkot after discovering that the "woman" he had met on social media was in fact a man looking for a same-sex relationship.

A Chinese corridor to India's east? China plans Bangladesh route after Pak CPECAfter pouring billions of dollars into the CPEC in Pakistan, Beijing is now planning a corridor on India's eastern flank through Bangladesh and Myanmar. During Bangladeshi PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, Beijing proposed the construction of a multi-modal trade route to Bangladesh. What does it mean for India?

Que sera, sera: Manish Tewari's cryptic post after Congress snub in PunjabManish Tewari posted a cryptic message after the Congress named its new Punjab organisational team. His remarks drew attention because he was missing from the line-up despite the party consulting several other senior leaders.

sports.headline: Galle, Colombo to host India in August Test series. Sri Lanka announces schedule Sri Lanka Cricket has confirmed Galle and Colombo as the venues for India's two-Test series in August. The matches carry World Test Championship importance for India as tougher assignments follow.

Shah Rukh Khan unveils new Los Angeles cricket stadium, thanks Jay Shah for support Shah Rukh Khan unveiled the Knight Riders Cricket Ground in Los Angeles, thanking Jay Shah and the ICC for support. The venue strengthens Major League Cricket in Southern California before cricket's LA28 Olympic return.

Sooryavanshi would have taken their pants off: Shastri's blunt verdict goes viral Ravi Shastri has slammed India's decision to leave Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench, insisting the 15-year-old should have debuted against Ireland and claiming he "would have taken their pants off" on a slow, small ground.

15-year-old made to wait: What exactly is India's plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi? India tour of England: Fast-tracked into the senior squad following a historic IPL campaign, the decision to leave 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench invites a compelling question over India's delicate balance between hierarchy and current merit.

Balogun did not deserve a red card: USA coach questions referee's decision United States coach Mauricio Pochettino launched a passionate defence of Folarin Balogun after the striker's controversial sending-off in the 2-0 World Cup round-of-32 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday.

Djokovic defends Serena Williams's Wimbledon return: Just enjoy her greatness Novak Djokovic has urged fans to celebrate Serena Williams's return to Wimbledon rather than rush to criticise her after the 44-year-old's comeback ended in a first-round defeat.

England out to break South Africa curse with Nat Sciver-Brunt boost for semis Women's T20 World Cup, ENG vs SA: England have lost their last two knockout matches against South Africa and will be eager to snap that streak when the two sides meet in the second semi-final at The Kennington Oval in London on Thursday.

Sooryavanshi's wait for India debut will put him under more pressure: Gavaskar Batting great Sunil Gavaskar believes delaying Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's senior India debut will only increase the pressure on the teenage opener, who was once again left out of the playing XI for the opening T20I against England on Wednesday.

10-man USA keep dream alive, reach last 16 with win over Bosnia & Herzegovina Malik Tillman spared the United States an anxious finish with a sublime late free-kick as the co-hosts defeated Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 on Wednesday to reach the World Cup Round of 16, despite playing the final stages with 10 men after Folarin Balogun's dismissal.

Heartbreaking moment as DR Congo coach learns of father's death after World Cup exit Moments after DR Congo's 1-2 defeat to England ended their historic World Cup run, coach Sebastien Desabre was informed of his father's death during the post-match press conference.

Thomas Tuchel urges parents to let children stay up for England's clash with Mexico England manager Thomas Tuchel may have slept through Mexico's victory over Ecuador, but after his side's dramatic 2-1 comeback win over DR Congo sealed a place in the World Cup Round of 16, he had a message for parents back home: let the children stay up to watch Sunday's showdown at the Azteca Stadium.

USA 2-0 BIH Live: Balogun, Tillman fire USA into Round of 16USA vs Bosnia & Herzegovina (BIH) Live, FIFA World Cup: Full-time: The United States are into the Round of 16 after a composed 2-0 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina in San Francisco. Folarin Balogun broke the deadlock late in the first half before Malik Tillman doubled the advantage after the interval to seal progression. The hosts also overcame Balogun's second-half red card, producing a disciplined defensive display to see out the contest with 10 men and book their place in the next round.

Senegal's night of promise ends in heartbreak as Belgium pull off the great escapeBelgium completed a stunning comeback from 2-0 down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time, with Youri Tielemans converting a controversial late VAR-awarded penalty. The Lions of Teranga were left heartbroken after seeing a famous World Cup upset slip away.

Harry Kane on Lionel Messi's level? Gordon makes big GOAT comparison after England winFIFA World Cup 2026: Anthony Gordon hailed Harry Kane after the England captain's brace turned a fraught tie against DR Congo. The comeback win sent England into the last 16 against co-hosts Mexico and underlined Kane's authority.

Novak Djokovic pranks ball girl, then outclasses Stefanos Tsitsipas to reach Round 3Novak Djokovic entertained Centre Court with a playful prank on a ball girl before cruising past Stefanos Tsitsipas 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 to reach the third round of Wimbledon 2026 on Wednesday.

Harry Kane's rocket sinks DR Congo, breaks Pele's World Cup record in styleHarry Kane scored twice as England came from behind to beat DR Congo 2-1 and reach the Round of 16. His late winner took him past Pele's World Cup tally as DR Congo coach Sebastien Desabre hailed him as the world's best striker.

Shreyas Iyer's wait for first win as India captain prolongs after Durham T20I washoutENG vs IND, 1st T20I: Abhishek Sharma's blistering 59 and Shreyas Iyer's fluent 68 powered India to 189 for 7 in the opening T20I against England, but persistent rain prevented the hosts from beginning their chase in Durham.

Kohli training with Sanjay Bangar in viral video fuels England ODI return buzzVirat Kohli's training session with former India batting coach Sanjay Bangar has set social media abuzz ahead of the England ODI series. With his return nearing after retiring from Tests and T20Is, fans are hoping the hard work in the nets translates into another vintage Kohli chapter.

South Africa not at peak, but England will be under pressure: Laura WolvaardtWomen's T20 World Cup, ENG vs SA: Speaking to reporters ahead of the semi-final at The Oval, Laura Wolvaardt admitted that South Africa are yet to produce their best cricket in the tournament, but said her side would look to capitalise on the pressure England will be under in the knockout clash.

French Open champion Mirra Andreeva makes shock exit in Round 2Barbora Krejcikova battled back from a set down to defeat Mirra Andreeva 4-6, 7-5, 6-4, knocking the reigning French Open champion out in the second round of Wimbledon.

BEL vs SEN Highlights: Late controversial penalty sends Belgium past Senegal 3-2Belgium vs Senegal Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (BEL 3-2 SEN) Belgium staged a dramatic comeback to beat Senegal in a tense knockout clash, overturning a late deficit before edging a controversial extra-time finish. Senegal will feel hard done by after dominating large spells but falling short in the decisive moments.

Nat Sciver-Brunt plays down title talk as England seek to end World Cup droughtWomen's T20 World Cup, ENG vs SA: Speaking to the reporters before the semis against South Africa, Nat Sciver-Brunt said that England aren't worried about not winning world titles since 2017 and will go one match at a time to reach their goal.

Captain Kane to the rescue as England survive DR Congo scareFIFA World Cup 2026: Harry Kane rescued England from a major scare with a decisive brace, overturning a shock DR Congo lead in a tense World Cup knockout clash. Despite a disciplined and fearless performance from the Leopards, England's quality and late momentum shift secured their progression.

Abhishek Sharma scripts history, becomes fastest batter to 100 T20I sixesAbhishek Sharma scripted history by becoming the fastest batter to 100 T20I sixes, reaching the milestone in just 785 balls before smashing a blistering 59 off 24 against England in the first T20I.

Jannik Sinner fights past Nuno Borges, Aryna Sabalenka survives McCartney Kessler scareDefending champion Jannik Sinner and world No.1 Aryna Sabalenka advanced to the Wimbledon third round after overcoming stern second-round tests on Wednesday. Sinner beat Nuno Borges in straight sets, while Sabalenka saved four set points before beating McCartney Kessler.

No Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debut in Durham as India ignore Gavaskar's suggestionThe wait for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut continues after the 15-year-old was left out of the first T20I against England despite growing calls for his inclusion. India stuck with their World Cup-winning core, ignoring Sunil Gavaskar's advice to hand the teenager an immediate debut.

Naomi Osaka conquers Wimbledon nerves, keeps daughter's birthday plans intact4-time Grand Slam champion Naomi Osaka eased past Anastasia Gasanova to reach the Wimbledon third round before revealing her daughter's birthday had made her unusually nervous.

ENG 2 -1 DRC Highlights: Kane double seals comeback win for EnglandEngland (ENG) vs DR Congo (DRC) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ENG 2 - 1 DRC) Harry Kane's second-half brace inspired England to a hard-fought 2-1 comeback win over DR Congo after the Three Lions had spent much of the afternoon chasing the game. Anthony Gordon changed the contest off the bench with two assists as England booked a Round of 16 meeting with Mexico.

Sooryavanshi should write exams, play gully cricket: Ex-cricketer fires a warningFormer South Africa cricketer Daryll Cullinan has urged cricket to slow down on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi despite the clamour for his India debut. He argues the teenager needs space, exams and guidance before the IPL and online scrutiny harden brilliance into burden.

Injury blow for Serena Williams, doubles with sister Venus in doubtWimbledon 2026: Serena Williams suffered a knee injury scare after her first-round loss to Maya Joint in singles. The setback has cast doubt over her scheduled doubles return with Venus Williams later this week.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
